benchmark_scope_note: OPT-04 benchmark-only UI contract. This does not represent current app truth, does not authorize implementation, and must be refreshed against current source before any future product UI task.
user_goal: Help a reviewer quickly inspect command evidence, changed artifacts, validation status, and safety assertions before making a separate review decision.

components.name: Screen header
components.purpose: Show benchmark label, review packet title, result state, and local-only scope.

components.name: Validation summary
components.purpose: Show command count, failed command count, JSON validation state, and diff-check state.

components.name: Command results
components.purpose: List command ids with argv summaries, exit codes, stdout/stderr artifact references, and timing.

components.name: Changed artifacts
components.purpose: Show files changed or proposed by a task, with review status and raw evidence expectations.

components.name: Safety assertions
components.purpose: Make non-authority claims visible without treating them as semantic approval.

layout_rules: Use a compact workbench-style layout rather than a marketing or landing page layout.
Place result and scope at the top of the screen.
Keep validation summary visible before command details.
Keep command results and changed artifacts in adjacent review regions.
Keep safety assertions visually distinct from semantic acceptance.
Avoid nested cards and decorative-only layout elements.
Use stable table and list dimensions so changing status text does not shift the whole screen.

states.description: All recorded validation commands passed, with safety assertions still requiring reviewer verification.
At least one command failed; the failed command remains inspectable with stdout and stderr artifacts.
A referenced stdout, stderr, diff, session, or review packet artifact is missing or unreadable.
Changed files or requested actions appear outside the approved task boundary.
No commands or changed artifacts have been recorded yet.

accessibility_notes: Use semantic headings and tables for review structure.
Do not rely on color alone for success, warning, or failure states.
Keep contrast high enough for dense reviewer workflows.
Use descriptive labels for artifact references and status chips.
Ensure keyboard reading order follows header, validation summary, command results, changed artifacts, safety assertions.
acceptance_checks: A later implementation task must read current app source before coding.
A later implementation task must preserve the existing product/runtime authority boundary.
The screen must distinguish evidence collection success from semantic task approval.
The screen must expose command exit codes and stdout/stderr artifact references.
The screen must not imply GitHub write, live Bridge, Dispatcher, Runner, or Codex runtime authority.
The screen must present safety assertions as review evidence, not as automatic approval.
The UI must remain usable when one or more commands fail.
non_goals: Authorize live Bridge, Dispatcher, Runner, or Codex runtime task execution
Treat this mockup as current app truth
